text:
Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily who lived in a quaint village nestled among whispering forests. She loved collecting autumn leaves. Once every year, around the week before Halloween, they would turn fiery orange and rustlingly golden. Every afternoon she would spend hours arranging them in neat patterns on her porch.

One particular year, after picking her usual bounty of leaves, a peculiar squirrel named Oliver decided Lily's leaf pile was the perfect spot for a secret stash. Oliver gathered an impressive collection himself, and soon their piles were larger than life. Lily would gaze at her pile from admiring distances, imagining castles made of autumn leaves.

One evening, as Moonlit Owls sang in the trees, Lily's dog, Max, startled her by jumping onto her leaf pile. Curiosity getting the better of him, Max started rearranging the leaves into intricate designs on the blanket under him. Suddenly, he stumbled and rolled out of the pile, leaving a swirling vortex of leaves.
